steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The margin of error (2.91%) defines a symmetric interval around the sample estimate (66%). The plausible values for the true population percent are those within that interval.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Any value greater than 63.09% and less than 68.91%: 66% − 2.91% = 63.09% and 66% + 2.91% = 68.91%. Values between these bounds are plausible. ✓
  • Choice B — Any value greater than 63.09%: Ignores the upper bound — values above 68.91% are not supported by the margin of error. ✗
  • Choice C — Any value less than 63.09% or greater than 68.91%: These are exactly the values outside the confidence interval, not inside it. ✗
  • Choice D — Any value less than 68.91%: Ignores the lower bound — values below 63.09% are not supported. ✗
That is the correct answer.
